title: [Hatterene] Battle Guide
summary: A [Psychic]/[Fairy] Trick Room wallbreaker that [bounce]s hazards with [magic-bounce] while swinging 136 Special Attack on the slow side.
strengths: Base 136 SpA with [Psychic]/[Fairy] STAB covers [Dark], [Dragon], and [Fighting]
Base 29 Speed becomes top tier under [trick-room]
weaknesses: Loses all tempo the second Trick Room expires

recommendedMoves.reason: Main STAB that leve[rage]s 136 Special Attack
Flips the Speed axis so base 29 leads every turn

recommendedItems.reason: Blocks [taunt] and [encore] on the Trick Room setup turn

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Hatterene] is the [Psychic]/[Fairy] piece that anchors Trick Room teams in Champions MA. The 57/95/136 offensive frame already hits hard, but the real value comes from [magic-bounce] — every [stealth-rock], [taunt], [spore], and [will-o-wisp] the opponent throws comes right back at them for free.

## Strengths
136 SpA is still premium for doubles, and the [Psychic]/[Fairy] STAB combo cleanly threatens [Dark], [Dragon], [Fighting], and [Poison] at once. Base 29 Speed reads like a liability but becomes the top of the Speed tier under [trick-room], which is why the [quiet] nature drops it even further. [magic-bounce] alone forces hazard leads to rethink turn one, which is a tempo win before the first move is c[lick]ed.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The 2x weaknesses to [Steel], [Poison], and [Ghost] pile up fast. Gholdengo, Corviknight, and Metagross-style walls flip the matchup, and any [knock-off] or [u-turn] user chips through the 57 HP before Trick Room even goes up. Once Trick Room expires [Hatterene]'s 29 Speed is exposed and priority [Ghost] attackers clean up.

## Recommended Moveset
[psychic] is the locked STAB for its raw numbers, and [dazzling-gleam] is the spread [Fairy] STAB that doubles teams want. [mystical-fire] is mandatory cove[rage] — without it Gholdengo and Metagross wall the set completely. The fourth slot is [trick-room] itself, because [Hatterene] is both the setter and the main abuser. In [sing]les, swap [dazzling-gleam] for [psyshock] to break specially defensive walls.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[mental-herb] is the premium pick: on the Trick Room setup turn it [block]s [taunt] and [encore], which is exactly when [Hatterene] is most vulnerable. [leftovers] is the alternative for longer games where [magic-bounce] already does the turn-one work. The ability is locked to [magic-bounce]. Tera [Fairy] cranks [dazzling-gleam], while Tera [Water] erases [Steel] and [Poison] weaknesses and turns [Hatterene] into a genuine wall.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Hatterene] wants heavy slow wallbreakers behind it — anything that abuses inverted Speed. [Fighting] and [Ground] partners clear [Steel] answers like Gholdengo, a [Fairy]-resistant pivot covers [Poison] threats, and an [imprison] or [taunt] user punishes oppo[sing] Trick Room mirrors. A priority-denying [Fairy] or [Fighting] ally keeps [Ghost] [sucker-punch] lines from stealing kills.

## Play Patterns
Lead [Hatterene], c[lick] [trick-room] turn one, and start swinging 136 SpA attacks from turn two. Because [magic-bounce] is live, never switch out of a hazard lead — bait their c[lick] and [reflect] it. Save Tera for the turn a [Steel] or [Ghost] move is guaranteed so the swap to [Water] or [Fairy] flips the matchup. Do not c[lick] [psychic] into known [Dark] pivots; go [dazzling-gleam] for the safer line.
